skill.name: lark-whiteboard
skill.description: Create Lark whiteboard diagrams from user requirements, including architecture diagrams, flowcharts, mind maps, charts, and visual frameworks.
skill.summary: Generate, render, and optionally upload structured diagrams for Lark whiteboards.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high-risk patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown fences, DSL examples, SVG namespace URLs, placeholder IDs, and documentation text. The confirmed risks are operational: the skill instructs agents to run npx, node, npm, and lark-cli commands, write generated files, and optionally upload or overwrite Lark whiteboards after authorization. No prompt-injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found in the reviewed files.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Commands With Installation and Rendering Side Effects
medium_findings.description: The skill intentionally instructs agents to install or run @larksuite/whiteboard-cli, execute node scripts, and render files. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it requires user consent and command review because external packages and local process execution are involved.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and aligned with the diagram-rendering workflow. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the file also requires user consent before installing dependencies.

medium_findings.title: Networked Lark Whiteboard Update Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill documents lark-cli commands that fetch or update Lark documents and whiteboards. These commands can modify remote content, so publication should warn users to confirm tokens, permissions, and overwrite behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The upload and dry-run commands are directly shown and have real remote side effects. The guidance includes overwrite checks, so this is controlled but still material risk.

medium_findings.title: Generated Script Files Write Local JSON Artifacts
medium_findings.description: Several scene guides include Node.js templates that write generated diagram JSON files. This is expected for coordinate-heavy diagrams, but agents should keep outputs in the working directory and avoid using untrusted file paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem writes are visible in concrete script templates. They use fixed output filenames in examples, so the concern is bounded file creation rather than arbitrary path traversal.

low_findings.title: Static Ruby Backtick Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The numerous Ruby or shell backtick alerts come from Markdown code fences, inline code, DSL examples, and CLI documentation. No Ruby source or untrusted Ruby backtick execution was found in the reviewed context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown examples or schema snippets, not executable Ruby files. The same pattern repeats across documentation files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and C2 Keyword Alerts Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear to be triggered by words, diagram IDs, or SVG/XML examples rather than cryptographic operations. C2 alerts map to placeholder IDs such as d2-c2 and ITEM_C2, not command-and-control behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines contain schema constraints, placeholder node IDs, and labels. No hashing, encryption, beaconing, or remote control logic is present in those locations.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Alerts Are Relative Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The path traversal findings in SKILL.md are relative links to neighboring Lark skills for authentication and document guidance. They do not read files or construct paths at runtime.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant text is a Markdown link to another skill file and a reference note. There is no executable filesystem access at those lines.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Alerts Are Dense Markdown and Code Examples
low_findings.description: The entropy findings appear on documentation files containing Chinese text, code blocks, JSON examples, SVG strings, and tables. No binary, encrypted payload, or obfuscated executable content was found during review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are readable Markdown documentation. The entropy signal is plausible because the files mix natural language, tables, symbols, and code examples.

dangerous_patterns.title: Package Execution Through npx
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses npx to execute @larksuite/whiteboard-cli for rendering and conversion. Agents should show commands and obtain approval when installing or running external packages.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and central to the skill. The package name is fixed, which reduces injection risk but not package execution risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Whiteboard Overwrite Flow
dangerous_patterns.description: The upload workflow can overwrite a non-empty whiteboard after dry-run confirmation. This is not malicious, but it can destroy user content if safeguards are bypassed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The overwrite risk is explicitly described in the skill. The built-in dry-run and confirmation requirement mitigate but do not eliminate the risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Script Generation for Layout Calculations
dangerous_patterns.description: Several diagram types require writing and running local JavaScript scripts to compute layout. This is suitable for complex diagrams but should remain scoped to generated diagram artifacts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script requirement is documented and repeated in scene guides. The templates use local calculations and fixed outputs, so the risk is moderate.

content.value_statement: Teams often need clear diagrams inside Lark documents but manual layout is slow. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to generate render-ready whiteboard diagrams and upload them when authorized.
actual_capabilities: Routes common diagram requests to either Lark whiteboard DSL or Mermaid syntax.
Provides layout rules for architecture, organization, comparison, chart, funnel, pyramid, flywheel, fishbone, and treemap diagrams.
Defines whiteboard DSL node schemas for frames, shapes, text, connectors, SVG, images, and rich text.
Supplies script templates for diagrams that need calculated coordinates or proportions.
Documents rendering commands for the Lark whiteboard CLI and optional upload through lark-cli.
Includes pre-flight guidance before overwriting an existing Lark whiteboard.
limitations: Requires Node.js 18 or newer and the Lark whiteboard CLI to render diagrams.
Uploading to Lark requires valid Lark authentication and user or bot permissions.
Some complex diagrams require temporary script files and manual visual review after rendering.
Mermaid output supports less visual control than the Lark whiteboard DSL path.

use_cases.title: Document system architecture
use_cases.description: Create layered service, data, and integration diagrams for project documentation in Lark.

use_cases.title: Prepare business visuals
use_cases.description: Generate funnels, comparison matrices, milestones, and flywheels for planning and review meetings.

use_cases.title: Explain workflows quickly
use_cases.description: Turn process descriptions into flowcharts, mind maps, and sequence diagrams with Mermaid routing when appropriate.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple flowchart
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a process diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Lark whiteboard flowchart for this approval process: request submitted, manager review, finance review, approved or rejected.

prompt_templates.title: Build an architecture diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Lark whiteboard architecture diagram for a web app with browser clients, API gateway, auth service, order service, Redis cache, PostgreSQL, and message queue.

prompt_templates.title: Render a business chart
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a funnel diagram for our conversion stages: visits 100%, signups 42%, trials 18%, paid customers 7%. Render it and check that labels are readable.

prompt_templates.title: Upload to an existing board
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced Lark integration with overwrite protection
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a flywheel diagram for our growth loop and upload it to this Lark whiteboard token. Run a dry run first and ask before overwriting existing content.

output_examples.input: Create an architecture diagram for a SaaS billing system.
output_examples.output: A rendered whiteboard image with grouped layers for clients, API services, billing workers, databases, and external payment providers.

output_examples.input: Show the quarterly adoption funnel in Lark.
output_examples.output: A funnel visualization with proportional stages, readable labels, coordinated colors, and a PNG preview before upload.

output_examples.input: Turn this meeting process into a flowchart.
output_examples.output: A Mermaid-based flowchart rendered through the whiteboard CLI, with concise node labels and clear branch decisions.

best_practices: Confirm Lark upload targets and permissions before running update commands.
Render a PNG preview and inspect text fit, connector routing, and color contrast before delivery.
Use script templates only for diagrams that need calculated positions or proportions.
anti_patterns: Do not overwrite an existing whiteboard without a dry run and explicit user approval.
Do not handwrite coordinates for fishbone, flywheel, funnel, pyramid, chart, or treemap layouts when the guide requires scripts.
Do not mix Mermaid syntax with the Lark whiteboard DSL in the same input file.

faq.question: Does this skill require Lark access?
faq.answer: Rendering local previews does not require Lark access. Uploading to a Lark whiteboard requires authentication and permissions.

faq.question: Can it create Mermaid diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. It routes mind maps, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, pie charts, and flowcharts to Mermaid when appropriate.

faq.question: Can it update an existing whiteboard?
faq.answer: Yes, but it instructs the agent to run a dry run first and ask before overwriting non-empty boards.

faq.question: What tools must be installed?
faq.answer: The guide expects Node.js 18 or newer, @larksuite/whiteboard-cli, and lark-cli for Lark upload operations.

faq.question: Does it support charts?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for bar charts, line charts, funnels, pyramids, treemaps, milestones, and flywheels.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It can be published with warnings because it runs external CLI commands and can modify Lark whiteboards when authorized.
